title: OccAny: Generalized Unconstrained Urban 3D Occupancy
summary: This paper introduces OccAny, a novel framework addressing the poor generalization of existing 3D occupancy prediction methods by enabling unconstrained operation on out-of-domain, uncalibrated urban scenes using only monocular or sequential images. The core technical advancements include the introduction of Segmentation Forcing to refine occupancy prediction while enabling semantic mask output, and a Novel View Rendering pipeline for geometry completion via test-time augmentation. Experiments show that OccAny surpasses existing visual geometry foundation models on 3D occupancy prediction while maintaining competitive results against specialized in-domain methods across multiple input modalities. The model achieves robust metric occupancy and segmentation feature prediction in challenging, unconstrained environments.

title: Active learning-enabled multi-objective design of thermally conductive and mechanically compliant polymers
summary: This paper introduces an active learning (AL) framework powered by multi-objective Bayesian optimization (MOBO) to efficiently design novel polymers balancing high thermal conductivity (TC) and low mechanical modulus. The methodology begins with a Molecular Dynamics (MD) pipeline to establish an initial dataset, upon which independent Deep Kernel Learning (DKL) surrogate models are trained for property prediction and uncertainty quantification. The system iteratively uses the parallel noisy expected hypervolume improvement (qNEHVI) acquisition function to guide the selection of new candidates for MD simulation, significantly accelerating the discovery process under data scarcity. The framework successfully identified six Pareto-optimal polymer candidates, demonstrating a reduced development time and providing actionable insights for synthesizing multifunctional materials.

title: Foveated Diffusion: Efficient Spatially Adaptive Image and Video Generation
summary: This work addresses the increasing computational cost of high-resolution image and video generation with diffusion models by exploiting the non-uniform acuity of human vision. The proposed Foveated Diffusion method uses a gaze-dependent mask to generate content using a mixed-resolution token setting, allocating more tokens to the high-detail foveal region. A key technical contribution is a mechanism to construct these mixed-resolution tokens consistently, allowing an existing base diffusion model to be efficiently post-trained for foveated generation. Evaluations show that this technique maintains perceptual quality comparable to full-resolution generation while significantly reducing computational complexity and generation time.

title: Dynamic Light Spanners in Doubling Metrics
summary: This paper introduces an efficient dynamic algorithm for maintaining a $(1+\varepsilon)$-spanner for a set of points undergoing insertions and deletions within a metric space that possesses a constant doubling dimension. The core innovation lies in achieving update times that are polylogarithmic in the aspect ratio $\Phi$ of the point set, specifically $\operatorname{poly}(\log \Phi)$. Crucially, the algorithm maintains a spanner whose total weight is bounded by a constant factor times the weight of the Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) of the current point set. This work resolves an open problem by providing the first efficient dynamic structure for light-weight spanners in this setting, including low-dimensional Euclidean spaces.

title: One View Is Enough! Monocular Training for In-the-Wild Novel View Generation
summary: This paper introduces OVIE, a novel framework for in-the-wild novel view synthesis that breaks the dependency on multi-view training supervision by utilizing only single, unpaired internet images. The core methodology involves using a monocular depth estimator as a transient geometric scaffold to lift source images into 3D space, apply view transformations, and generate pseudo-target views for training. To handle the inherent issues of single-view reconstruction, such as occlusions, the authors propose a masked training loss formulation restricting metrics to geometrically valid regions. OVIE demonstrates superior zero-shot performance against existing methods while boasting a 600x faster inference time, as the geometry estimation is discarded post-training.

title: Stablecoins as Dry Powder: A Copula-Based Risk Analysis of Cryptocurrency Markets
summary: This research investigates the systemic risk transmission role of stablecoins within the cryptocurrency ecosystem using copula-based econometric analysis. The authors quantify the flow of volatility and trading activity from stablecoins into the broader crypto market, highlighting stablecoins' function as 'dry powder.' Key findings show demonstrable Granger causality across multiple time horizons and significant improvements in cryptocurrency return forecasting accuracy (lower MSE) when stablecoin factors are included. Economically, utilizing these factors leads to reduced risk in volatility targeting strategies.

title: Statistical Efficiency of Single- and Multi-step Models for Forecasting and Control
summary: This paper provides a foundational quantitative analysis of the statistical efficiency trade-off between single-step and multi-step time series predictors when applied to linear dynamical systems. The authors investigate three training regimes: single-step models, direct multi-step models, and single-step models trained with multi-step losses. They theoretically establish that if the model class perfectly matches the system dynamics, single-step models yield the lowest asymptotic prediction error. Conversely, when partial observability introduces model misspecification, direct multi-step predictors demonstrate superior accuracy by effectively reducing systemic bias. These established prediction trade-offs are shown to hold when the predictors are utilized within a closed-loop control framework.

title: PNap: Lifecycle-aware Edge Multi-state sleep for Energy Efficient MEC
summary: This paper addresses the significant energy consumption in Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) systems caused by maintaining unnecessary active servers during low-load periods. The proposed solution, PowerNap (PNap), is a lifecycle-aware orchestration framework that coordinates server sleep states with the required service lifecycle operations. By integrating traffic forecasting, PNap dynamically decides when to transition servers to deeper sleep states, thereby minimizing the active server count while preventing service disruptions. Experimental validation shows PNap achieves up to a 14.9% reduction in energy consumption compared to existing state-of-the-art methods while preserving service availability metrics.

title: Granger Causality in Expectiles: an M-vine copula test
summary: This paper introduces a model-free measure of Granger causality specifically tailored to expectiles, offering a robust generalization of mean-based causality that is ideal for risk quantification and distributional analysis. The authors construct a powerful statistical test based on M-vine copulas, which effectively models complex, non-linear, and non-Gaussian dependencies across multiple time series without imposing restrictive parametric forms on the joint distribution. The proposed test is shown to be strongly consistent and exhibits good finite-sample performance, notably revealing multivariate causal structures overlooked by pairwise methods. Practical relevance is highlighted through applications to international stock market indices.

title: WaveSFNet: A Wavelet-Based Codec and Spatial--Frequency Dual-Domain Gating Network for Spatiotemporal Prediction
summary: WaveSFNet addresses the challenge of multi-step spatiotemporal prediction by developing an efficient, recurrent-free framework that balances modeling long-range dynamics with preserving sharp, high-frequency details. The core innovation is a wavelet-based codec integrated with a dual-domain gated spatiotemporal translator. The translator first injects adjacent-frame differences to emphasize motion, and then fuses information via a novel mechanism that combines large-kernel spatial modeling with frequency-domain global modulation. Experimental results across Moving MNIST, TaxiBJ, and WeatherBench demonstrate that WaveSFNet maintains high prediction accuracy while exhibiting low computational overhead.
